strengths: 17+ years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — meets the 15-year minimum with breadth across stacks
Founder of GISCollective (2016–present), an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes and used in production by NGOs — demonstrates entrepreneurial execution inside a longer career
Current role at re:cinq includes AI-agent infrastructure work: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks, showing early positioning toward AI tooling
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field — holds two bachelor's degrees (Computer Science and Fine Arts), which falls below the preferred academic profile for a director-level AI strategy role
AI expertise is shallow and recent: the only AI work appears in the current role (April 2025–present), with no demonstrated AI products or platforms shipped at scale prior to that
No evidence of strategic advisory experience — no record of advising C-suite, strategic customers, or partners on cloud/AI adoption, which is a core responsibility of this principal-level IC role
response.justification: The role requires recognized AI expertise with demonstrated products or platforms — the candidate's only AI-adjacent work (MCP tooling, agentic frameworks) began in April 2025, providing less than two months of evidence at the time of the résumé. While the breadth of engineering history and the GISCollective founding are genuine positives, neither rises to the level of cloud-platform-scale AI product ownership or executive-facing advisory work that a director-level AI strategy IC must demonstrate. The absence of a graduate degree in AI and the lack of any strategic consulting or partner-advisory track record compound the gap. Score is derived from a strong engineering foundation offset by critical absences in AI depth, advisory capability, and academic credential.
